login.title: Login
login.error_required: Please enter your username and password.

devices.title: Devices
devices.no_devices_desc: Devices appear here once they connect and send a heartbeat.

sessions.title: Active Sessions
sessions.empty_desc: Sessions appear here while remote connections are in progress

addressbook.title: Address Book
addressbook.no_book_desc: This user's address book will be created when they first log in.
addressbook.no_peers: No peers in this address book yet.
addressbook.pick_device_hint: Selecting a device auto-fills the fields below. You can still edit them.

groups.title: Device Groups
groups.no_groups_desc: Create groups to organise your devices and control which users can see them.
groups.confirm_delete: Delete group '%s'? Devices will be unassigned and members removed.
groups.members_desc: Members of this group can see devices assigned to it.
groups.visibility_desc: Users only see devices that belong to groups they are members of, plus their own registered devices and any ungrouped devices. Admins always see everything. Knowing a Device ID still allows a direct connection regardless of group membership.

audit.title: Audit Log
audit.subtitle: Connection and file transfer events
audit.no_events_desc: Connection and file-transfer events will appear here.

server.title: Client Setup Guide
server.http_warning: you are running without SSL. All traffic between the RustDesk client and this API is unencrypted. This is fine for a trusted home network or VLAN; for anything internet-facing, consider adding a domain and Nginx Proxy Manager with a free Let's Encrypt certificate.
server.api_url_hint: enter this in the RustDesk client Network settings
server.key_not_found: Public key file not found at %s.
server.key_not_found_hint: Make sure the ./data volume is mounted correctly and hbbs has started at least once.
server.step1_desc: Download the latest standard RustDesk client from rustdesk.com/download. No Pro licence or custom build is required — the standard client works with SkonaDesk out of the box.
server.step2_desc: In the RustDesk client: click the three-dot menu (⋮) → Network → unlock if required.
server.step3_ssl_note: The API Server field is the full HTTPS URL above. RustDesk contacts it on port 443 via Nginx Proxy Manager.
server.step3_http_note: HTTP mode: The API Server field must include the port, e.g. %s. The RustDesk client contacts it directly — no proxy required.
server.step4_desc: In the RustDesk client click the account icon (top right) and sign in with your SkonaDesk username and password. Your address book and groups will sync automatically.
server.step4_warning: Important: The machine initiating the connection must be logged in. The machine being connected to does not need to be.
server.step5_desc: Enter another machine's Device ID and click Connect. If the connection fails, check the Server page and verify the firewall ports below are open.
server.firewall_note: All ports must be open on both your VPS firewall/security group and any upstream router or hosting panel. Port 443 is handled by Nginx Proxy Manager — no direct exposure of the API container needed.
server.trouble_key_mismatch_desc: The key shown above changed (e.g. after a volume wipe). Clear the RustDesk client settings (Network → reset), re-enter the server details and key, then reconnect.
server.trouble_secure_tcp: Failed to secure tcp / Device offline when logged in
server.trouble_secure_tcp_desc: This is the bug SkonaDesk was built to fix. If you are seeing it, the hbbs container may be running the unpatched rustdesk/rustdesk-server image rather than skonadesk-hbbs. Check the Server page for the running image version.
server.trouble_sync_desc_ssl: Check that Nginx Proxy Manager has a proxy host pointing %s → skonadesk-api:21114 with a valid SSL certificate.
server.trouble_sync_desc_http: Check that port 21114 is open on the server firewall and that API_PUBLIC_URL in your .env matches the IP/port your clients can reach.
server.trouble_lan: Can connect on LAN but not remotely
server.trouble_lan_desc: Ports 21115–21119 are not open on the VPS firewall or hosting control panel. Check all five ports in the table above — both TCP and UDP on 21116.
server.relay_desc: Relay (hbbr) — fallback traffic when P2P fails
server.copy_key_desc: Copy this key into the RustDesk client's Key field under Network settings to ensure you're connecting to this specific server.
server.setup_desc: Configure any RustDesk client to connect to this server by following these steps:
server.step_key_desc: Paste the Public Key shown above into the Key field. Leave blank if you skip key verification (less secure).
server.quick_config_desc: Skip manual entry — download a ready-made config file or run the one-liner for your platform. Close RustDesk before applying, then reopen it.
server.download_toml_desc: Works on Windows, Linux & macOS — just drop it in the right folder (shown below).
server.powershell_heading: Run in PowerShell (right-click → Run as Administrator not required unless RustDesk is installed system-wide):
server.preconfigured_desc: RustDesk supports embedding server config directly into the client filename. Download the standard RustDesk installer, rename it using the string below, and distribute it — opening or installing it will automatically apply your server settings. No manual configuration required for your users.
server.preconfigured_rename: Rename the downloaded installer to match your platform:
server.platform_macos: macOS — use the import string below instead (app bundle renaming is unreliable)
server.universal_import: Universal: paste the config string into RustDesk → Network → ID/Relay Server → Import on any platform:
server.relay_note: The relay field is intentionally left blank — when host and relay are the same server, RustDesk auto-detects the relay.
server.security_key_check_desc: Standard RustDesk binaries embed a hardcoded key that never matches a custom server. The key check is bypassed — auth is handled by JWT login instead.
server.security_transport_http: API is unencrypted (direct IP). For production use, place behind a reverse proxy with TLS.
server.security_transport_https: API protected by TLS via Nginx Proxy Manager.
server.security_e2e_desc: Peer-to-peer sessions use RustDesk's built-in E2E encryption — the server never sees session content.
server.security_relay_auth_desc: JWT token validated server-side in patched hbbs. Connections from clients without a valid login token are rejected at the rendezvous layer before any relay bandwidth is used. Only the initiating side must be logged in — the callee does not need to be.
server.matrix_desc: Not every machine needs the same configuration. Verified against the patched hbbs source. Peer-to-peer session content is always E2E encrypted regardless — the key only affects the rendezvous metadata channel.
server.matrix_row_caller_encrypted_callee_plain: Works — caller encrypted, callee plaintext rendezvous
server.matrix_row_callee_encrypted_caller_plain: Works — callee encrypted, caller plaintext rendezvous
server.matrix_row_caller_not_logged: Blocked — caller not logged in, rejected at rendezvous
server.matrix_row_callee_not_registered: Blocked — callee not registered, appears offline
server.matrix_row_caller_no_server: Blocked — caller cannot reach rendezvous server
server.matrix_legend_login: Login = logged in to SkonaDesk API via RustDesk client
server.matrix_legend_key: Key = server public key configured in RustDesk Network settings
server.firewall_port_direct: API Server (direct access — no reverse proxy)

profile.title: My Profile
profile.password_too_short: New password must be at least 8 characters.

settings.title: Settings
settings.default_language_hint: Default language used for new users and the login page.
